DECLARATION OF DAVID R. DONADIO PURSUANT TO C.C.P. 36.5 IN SUPPORT OF MOTION FOR ORDER GRANTING PREFERENCE IN SETTING CASE FOR TRIAL, AND EXTENDING DISCOVERY CUTOFF (TRANSACTION ID # 78692229) FILED BY PLAINTIFF BELL, PRAYTHER
MINI MINUTES FOR APR-14-26 09:00 AM FOR DEPT 304

ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION 304, NOT CONTESTED, NO APPEARANCES, AND TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED AS FOLLOWS: PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR ORDER GRANTING PREFERENCE IN SETTING CASE FOR TRIAL, AND EXTENDING DISCOVERY CUTOFF IS GRANTED UNDER C.C.P. SS 36(A). CONDITIONAL NON-OPPOSITION FILED BY DEFENDANT MORSE TEC LLC, F/K/A BORGWARNER MORSE TEC LLC, AS SUCCESSOR-BY MERGER TO BORG-WARNER CORPORATION. REPLY FILED. THE TRIAL DATE IS AUGUST 3, 2026, AT 11:15 A.M. IN DEPARTMENT 206. LAST DAY PURSUANT TO C.C.P. SECTION 36(F) IS AUGUST 12, 2026. THE LAST DAY FOR HEARING SUMMARY JUDGMENT/ADJUDICATION MOTIONS IS JULY 28, 2026. A FURTHER CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE IS SET FOR JUNE 16, 2026, AT 4:00 P.M. A MANDATORY SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE IS SET FOR THURSDAY, JULY 30, 2026, AT 9:30 A.M. IN DEPARTMENT 305. COURT TO REVIEW THE PROPOSED ORDER SUBMITTED BY COUNSEL FOR PLAINTIFF. JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN. CLERK: JACQUELINE ALAMEDA. BAILIFF: NOT PRESENT. NOT REPORTED. (304)

calendar.matters:
ASBESTOS TRIAL SETTING CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Apr-14-2026 Set For Trial On Aug-03-26 At 11:15 A.M. In Dept 206

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE - TRIAL COURTS
Off Calendar May-19-2026 Continued To Jun-16-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 502

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Granting Preference In Setting Case For Trial, And Extending Discovery Cutoff
Off Calendar Apr-14-2026 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Not contested, no appearances, and tentative ruling adopted as follows:

Plaintiff's Motion for Order Granting Preference in Setting Case for Trial, and Extending Discovery Cutoff is GRANTED under C.C.P. SS 36(a). Conditional Non-Opposition filed by Defendant Morse Tec LLC, f/k/a BorgWarner Morse TEC LLC, as successor-by merger to Borg-Warner Corporation. Reply filed.

The Court will not impose conditions on Plaintiff's right to a preferential trial date. "Where a party meets the requisite standard for calendar preference under [Code of Civil Procedure section 36] subdivision (a), preference must be granted. No weighing of interests is involved." (Fox v. Superior Court (2018) 21 Cal.App.5th 529, 535; see also, e.g., Miller v. Superior Court (1990) 221 Cal.App.3d 1200, 1204 [statute "grants a mandatory and absolute right to trial preference"]; Swaithes v. Superior Court (1989) 212 Cal.App.3d 1082, 1085 [trial court "has no power to balance the different interests of opposing litigants in applying the provision"]; Koch-Ash v. Superior Court (1986) 180 Cal.App.3d 689, 694 [section 36(a) "must be deemed to be mandatory and absolute" and "no discretion is left to trial courts."].)

1. The trial date is August 3, 2026, at 11:15 a.m. in Department 206.
a. Last day pursuant to C.C.P. section 36(f) is August 12, 2026.
b. The parties shall follow the California Rules of Court, San Francisco Local Rules, and Local Rule 20.

2. The last day for hearing summary judgment/adjudication motions is July 28, 2026.
a. Summary judgment/adjudication motions shall be brought on regular notice pursuant to the relevant provisions of the C.C.P., unless the parties stipulate otherwise.
b. Before a party files and serves a summary judgment/adjudication motion, it must contact the clerk to make a reservation.
c. The Court allows a maximum of four summary judgment/adjudication motions per day to be calendared, unless good cause is found to exceed this number. Contact the clerk to schedule a good cause hearing.

3. Time to respond to written discovery not yet served is shortened to 20 days.
a. For written discovery that has already been served, responses are due within 20 days of this hearing or by the date determined by the C.C.P., whichever is earlier.
b. Any issue/dispute that requires meet and confer, shall occur in person or via telephone, not by email or letter.

4. Electronic service is considered the equivalent of personal service.
5. The fact discovery cut-off date is July 17, 2026.
6. The expert discovery cut-off date is July 31, 2026.

7. All bankruptcy documents shall be turned over to defendants no later than May 14, 2026.
a. If Plaintiff submits documents to bankruptcy trusts after this date, they must notify Defendants no later than five days after submission.
b. The fact discovery cut-off does not apply to bankruptcy documents.

(Part 1 of 2, mini minutes continue in Part 2 of 2)

PLAINTIFF PRAYTHER BELL NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION FOR ORDER GRANTING PREFERENCE IN SETTING CASE FOR TRIAL, AND EXTENDING DISCOVERY CUTOFF - FOR TENTATIVE RULING PURPOSES ONLY
Off Calendar Apr-14-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
(Part 2 of 2, mini minutes continue from previous entry)

8. No later than April 28, 2026:
a. Plaintiff must provide a statement to each Defendant stating the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting Plaintiff's claims as to each Defendant.
b. Defendants must provide a statement stating the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of facts supporting Defendant's affirmative defenses.
c. Any person not identified on the statements may be barred from testifying at trial or presenting a declaration on a summary judgment motion.

9. A further case management conference is set for June 16, 2026, at 4:00 p.m.
a. Case management conference statements are not required.
b. Parties may appear at the conference by zoom.
c. If the parties agree that no CMC is necessary, they shall notify the Court at least two court days before the hearing.

10. Plaintiffs will send a settlement demand to Defendants no later than June 22, 2026. Response to the settlement demand is due July 6, 2026. The parties shall separately submit confidential settlement demands/offers. This confidential value must be separately submitted to Department305@sftc.org via electronic mail on July 13, 2026.

11. A mandatory settlement conference is set for Thursday, July 30, 2026, at 9:30 a.m. in Department 305. Personal appearance is required by all unless a party is excused for good cause. A request to excuse a personal appearance must be submitted to Department305@sftc.org two weeks before the mandatory settlement conference.

Court to review the proposed order submitted by counsel for Plaintiff.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Not Present. Not Reported. (304)
